items.title: Trace agent runs with OpenTelemetry gen_ai spans end to end
items.why: Without nested spans for LLM calls, tools, and retrieval you cannot tell which step failed, looped, or blew the token budget in production.
how_to_steps: Install OpenTelemetry SDK and auto-instrument HTTP clients (HTTPx/FastAPI or your stack's equivalents).
Wrap each agent run in a root span; add child spans per LLM call, tool invocation, and retrieval step.
Set `gen_ai.request.model`, token usage attributes, and propagate W3C `traceparent` across MCP/tool calls.
Export via OTLP to a collector with tail-based sampling (keep 100% of errors/slow traces).
items.evidence: Red Hat's agentic-workflow quickstart traces requests across app workloads, MCP servers, and Llama Stack; Geodocs spec defines gen_ai attribute set for vendor-neutral OTLP export.
items.try_today: Add one manual span around your agent's main LLM call and confirm it appears in your OTLP backend.

items.title: Execute untrusted agent-generated code in Vercel Sandbox microVMs
items.why: Letting agents run shell commands on your laptop or CI host risks credential leaks; isolated Firecracker VMs contain side effects while still running real builds and tests.
how_to_steps: Install `@vercel/sandbox` and authenticate with `vercel link` + OIDC token (or access token for external CI).
Create a sandbox per agent task: `Sandbox.create({ runtime: 'node24', timeout: ms('5m') })`.
Seed workspace files, run commands via `sandbox.runCommand`, capture stdout/stderr only from the VM.
Snapshot after dependency install so subsequent runs skip setup; destroy sandbox when the task completes.
items.evidence: Vercel Sandbox GA blog: sub-second starts, active-CPU billing, Blackbox dispatches parallel agents each in an isolated sandbox without resource contention.
items.try_today: Run one agent-generated script inside a Sandbox microVM instead of on your host and compare isolation setup time.
